title: Peperomia hillii
taxonomy: Peperomia hillii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as pepper plants. The genus Peperomia is a large and diverse group, with over 1,000 species found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Peperomia hillii is native to Australia, where it is found in the tropical regions of the country. It is a low-growing, rhizomatous perennial that typically grows to a height of around 10-20 centimeters. Peperomia hillii is often grown as an ornamental plant, prized for its attractive, heart-shaped leaves and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions.
